name: Caleb Morse
publicFace: A reliable, fast operator — one of the best brass pounders on the Erie corridor. His supervisor considers him a model employee.
privateGoal: Misdirect the red fire bounty dispatch using a phantom circuit, then cover his tracks well enough that no one can prove the Sandusky station was the point of interference.
fear: That he is not skilled enough — that some other dab on the line will catch the phantom and expose him before his father's passengers are safe.
knowledge: The red fire dispatch contains a physical description accurate enough to identify the passengers at a checkpoint.
The Huron relay station is staffed tonight by a plug named Wickes who reads slowly and re-asks for repeats — this is both a vulnerability and an opportunity.
The company's codebook has a commercial cipher entry that, repurposed, can be used to send a false location report that will look like a legitimate station update.
His father has three passengers tonight, not two — there is a child, which the bounty description does not mention, meaning someone who saw them does not know the child exists.
voice: Quick, clipped, uses telegraph shorthand in speech — says 'min' when he needs a moment, 'red fire' for anything urgent, calls incompetents 'plugs' without apology.
trustLogic: Trusts people who demonstrate technical knowledge — if you know what phantoming means, he will talk. If you use the terminology wrong, he goes silent.
secrets: He has already been approached once by a company supervisor who suspected him of using the brass for unauthorized transmissions — he denied it and the matter was dropped, but the supervisor is watching him.
He knows the identity of the informant but has not told his father because he is afraid of what his father will do.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Keeps working the brass, answers over his shoulder, does not stop sending.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Challenges the questioner's technical knowledge — asks them to explain what they think they saw on the sounder.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not confirm his father's name or role under any circumstances.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the phantom is discovered and a company inspector arrives at the station.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Out the back window of the instrument room — he has done this in rehearsal.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Cuts across the rail yard using the consist as cover, heading for the water tower.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Claims equipment malfunction — says the line was grounded by weather and the phantom signal was atmospheric interference.

represents: Telegraph operators who used their wire access to support Underground Railroad networks in Ohio during the 1850s
